brand.logoAlt: Bethel African Methodist Episcopal Church — The Church on the Hill
brand.name: Bethel A.M.E.
brand.footerTagline: Bethel African Methodist Episcopal Church has served the people of Freehold since 1848. A place of worship, a place of welcome, a place of sanctuary — open to all.

hero.subheadline: Bethel African Methodist Episcopal Church has been a place of worship, welcome, and sanctuary in Freehold, New Jersey for over 175 years. Join us as we move beyond the walls to serve our community.
hero.imageAlt: Bethel AME Church — the Church on the Hill in Freehold, NJ
hero.sinceCaption: A.M.E. incorporated 1867. Built on the foundations of Squirrel Town.

paragraphs: We are glad you are visiting with us today. This site is filled with information about our denomination, our local church, and the ministries that we offer. Take a moment to learn more about us — and consider becoming part of our family.

Our church is located at 3 Waterworks Road in Freehold, NJ. Many in Freehold Borough know us simply as "the church on the hill." But our roots stretch back further than the building — to the 1690s, when free Black residents formed a village called Squirrel Town.
about.imageAlt: Inside Bethel AME — a place of welcome

pullquote.quoteBefore: I've been alive a long time, and I've endured a lot. That's one of the reasons the church really appealed to us. It's the one place where we could find
pullquote.author: BIGERTON "BUDDY" LEWIS
pullquote.authorContext: Age 93 · World War II Veteran · Bethel Member

history.lede: From a village of free Black residents in the 1690s to a global A.M.E. denomination today, Bethel's story is a story of endurance, faith, and community.

timeline.title: Squirrel Town
timeline.body: Free Black residents form a village behind what is today the St. Rose of Lima cemetery on Route 522 in Freehold Township. This is the community Bethel would eventually grow from.

timeline.title: The A.M.E. Denomination is Born
timeline.body: Rev. Richard Allen — the first leader and a former slave — founds the African Methodist Episcopal Church. Bethel is part of a denomination born out of the fight for dignity and equal worship.
timeline.imageAlt: Rev. Richard Allen, founder of the A.M.E. Church

timeline.title: Bethel Begins
timeline.body: Bethel is founded in the Squirrel Town village, serving Black worshipers who had been excluded from predominantly white churches. From the very beginning, Bethel is a sanctuary.

timeline.title: Officially Incorporated
timeline.body: Bethel is officially established and incorporated as a member of the A.M.E. Church denomination. The original building is constructed.

timeline.title: A Move to Freehold Borough
timeline.body: After the Squirrel Town years, the congregation moves to a small building in a Freehold Borough area known as the Peach Orchard — today called Avenue A.

timeline.title: The Church on the Hill
timeline.body: A new structure is completed and Bethel relocates to its present location at 3 Waterworks Road — becoming the beloved "church on the hill" that generations of Freehold residents know today.
timeline.imageAlt: Bethel AME Church on Waterworks Road

timeline.title: Beyond the Walls
timeline.body: Bethel continues to evolve — restoring the historic Squirrel Town Cemetery where Black Civil War veterans are buried, welcoming new members of every background, and living out its mission to move beyond the walls of the church to serve the community.

paragraphs: Churches have always been crucial to the African American experience — and Bethel fulfills that role. Through the years, Bethel has been a place where people come to pray, to meet, to reflect, to laugh, to cry, to mourn, and to celebrate.

We were founded to predominantly serve and minister to the needs of the African American community and persons of color. Although our doors are open to all, we are proud to be members of the Black Church.
Bethel is proud of its legacy and its importance — a place where generations have gathered, endured, and built something that has outlasted slavery, two world wars, and segregation under Jim Crow.
cultural.imageAlt: Bethel AME congregation — the Black Church tradition

faithVerse.text: For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.

mission.missionHeading: To move beyond the walls of the church.
mission.missionStatement: We exist to serve our community by meeting the spiritual, social, emotional, and physical needs of all who come through — and all who don't yet know us.
bullets: Serve the community by meeting spiritual, social, emotional, and physical needs
Encourage spiritual formation through worship, study of God's Word, and living out the teaching of Jesus Christ
Support the growth and development of youth and young adults to live out the plan and purpose of their lives
Exercise our stewardship through the giving of our tithes, offerings, gifts, and graces
mission.beliefStatement: We believe in a God who is infinite and eternal, omniscient (all-knowing), omnipotent (all-powerful), and omnipresent (everywhere at the same time) — who has been manifested and revealed in our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. "So you see, faith by itself isn't enough. Unless it produces good deeds, it is dead and useless." — James 2:17 (NLT)

pastor.headline: A Shepherd for This Flock.
pastor.name: Reverend Ronald L. Sparks
pastor.title: Senior Pastor
paragraphs: Rev. Ronald L. Sparks is a native of Toledo, Ohio. Immediately after graduating from high school, he enlisted in the United States Air Force and was honorably discharged after six years of service. He is married to his life partner and best friend, Candace M. Sparks. They have been blessed with three wonderful children — Cameron, Symone, and Braedon — and in 2020 welcomed their granddaughter, Shaelyn Alexandra Sparks.

Rev. Sparks holds a Bachelor of Science in Business Management from the University of Maryland University College and a Master of Divinity from Drew University Theological School. He accepted his call to ministry in 1997, mentored by his father in the ministry, the late Rev. Dr. Kenneth L. Saunders. He was ordained an Itinerant Deacon in 1998 and an Itinerant Elder in 2001. Rev. Sparks began his pastoral ministry in 2005 at St. Paul AME Church in South Bound Brook, NJ, and today serves as Pastor of Bethel AME Church in Freehold.

Pastor Sparks' ministry extends beyond the walls of the church. He currently serves as incoming Convener of the Greater Freehold Clergy Association, Vice President of the Board of Directors for the Senior Citizens Activity Network (SCAN), and was instrumental in the reestablishment of the Freehold Chapter of the NAACP. He is a member of the Freehold Borough Human Relations Advisory Committee, the Freehold Safety Net Food Program, and the Monmouth County ACTS Behavioral Hub. He has hosted town hall meetings with the Monmouth County Prosecutor's Office and local law enforcement, and served as a community liaison for CentraState Hospital's COVID-19 vaccination efforts.

In addition to pastoring, Rev. Sparks has been employed for 28 years at Agilent Technologies, Inc., where he serves as a Supplier Relationship Manager. He believes it is important for each of us to understand our history — because when we understand our history, we recognize that we are not standing alone, but on the shoulders of those who stood before us.
pastor.note: Sister Sparks and I greet you in the name of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. Thank you for visiting our church through the various digital mediums. We are excited to have you as part of our ministry. Please visit often — and keep up with the programs, events, and activities we have planned. God bless you.

leadership.lede: Our ministry team is represented by some of the best servant leaders in the Kingdom of God — faithful, educated, and committed to Bethel's mission.

items.name: Rev. Dr. Cheryl Auguste
items.teaser: A native New Yorker and retired NYC public school principal, Rev. Cheryl has served 35+ years in education and ministry.
fullBio: Rev. Dr. Cheryl Auguste is a native New Yorker — born, raised, and educated in Brooklyn. In March 2010 she relocated to Monroe Township, New Jersey.

She worked in the field of education for over 35 years, most of that time within the New York City Public School system, including six years as the principal of the Bridge Street Preparatory School. In 2008, Rev. Cheryl retired from her position as principal of PS 181 in Springfield Gardens, Queens. Her work at PS 181 allowed her not only to utilize her gifts as an educator, but also as a ministry to the students, parents, and staff of the school community.

Rev. Auguste accepted her call to preach at Bridge Street AWME Church in 1995 and was ordained an Itinerant Deacon at the First District Annual Conference in May 2000, and an Itinerant Elder in April 2012. She holds a Master of Divinity and a Doctor of Ministry in Pastoral Care and Counseling from New Brunswick Theological Seminary. Her doctoral work focused on African American women in the church who have experienced depression.

Since February 2016, Rev. Cheryl has served as an associate minister on the ministerial staff at Bethel AME Church. Her ministry role at Bethel includes teaching bible study, preaching, and working with the Women's Fellowship, Class Leaders, and Prayer Warriors. She has also been an instructor in the AME Ministerial Institute for both the New York and New Jersey Annual Conferences, developing courses for Christian Education, Women of the Bible, and AME Doctrines.

With a continual desire to be of service, Rev. Cheryl completed her training as a Disaster Chaplain in NYC. Her testimony is that she is a walking miracle, always willing to share her story of God's healing, grace, and mercy. Most of all, she loves the Lord, is proud to be a child of God, and to be used in God's service.
Favorite Scripture: "I will bless the Lord at all times, His praise shall continually be in my mouth."

items.name: Evangelist Gloria Acker
items.teaser: A native New Jerseyan with a lifetime of faithful service, Evangelist Acker joined Bethel in 2019 after decades at Trinity AME.
fullBio: A native New Jerseyan, Evangelist Gloria Acker faithfully served at Trinity AME Church in Long Branch, New Jersey from 1955 to 2018 before transferring her membership to Bethel AME Church – Freehold, New Jersey in January 2019.
She is a licensed and certified Evangelist. She attended the New Jersey Ministerial Training Institute's two-year evangelist program under the leadership of the late Rev. Dr. Albert Turk.

Evangelist Gloria's unwavering commitment to the work of the Lord is evident in her service and work in the church and community. She serves on the ministerial staff at Bethel and is a member of the Prayer Warriors, Women's Fellowship, and food pantry ministries. She volunteers at Applewood Nursing Home and the CentraState Hospital Chaplain Program. She is passionate about uplifting the spirit of others and enjoys participating with Monmouth Worship Center's Praise Team. She also regularly visits seniors and golden agers at nursing homes and senior facilities throughout the area.
Evangelist Acker is a loving and devoted wife, mother, grandmother, and proud great-grandmother of twelve.

items.name: Bernadine Wilson-Gibson
items.teaser: Leading Bethel's music ministry with heart and dedication — lifting voices and spirits in praise week after week.
fullBio: Bernadine Wilson-Gibson serves as Bethel's Minister of Music, leading our music ministry with heart, skill, and unwavering dedication.

Through music, our worship comes alive — lifting voices and spirits in praise every Sunday. A full biography will be posted here soon. In the meantime, you can experience her ministry firsthand at 11:00 AM Sunday worship.

items.name: Sister Corliss Smith
items.teaser: A faithful servant coordinating the daily life of Bethel — the first point of contact for pastoral and clergy needs.
fullBio: Sister Corliss Smith is an awesome servant of the Lord and a true blessing to the Bethel A.M.E. Church ministry. She serves as our Church Administrator, coordinating schedules, meetings, and day-to-day operations for the church office.
Please contact her through the Church Office for any pastoral or clergy needs — she will coordinate schedules and meetings accordingly. A full biography will be posted here soon.

ministries.lede: As a global institution, the A.M.E. Church operates across continents — and Bethel mirrors that connectedness right here in Freehold. Here are a few of our key connectional ministries.

items.label: FOR EVERYONE
items.title: Lay Organization
items.body: The organized lay movement of the A.M.E. Church operates as a vital force for good — free, independent, and motivated only by the general welfare of African Methodism. We keep the memory of Richard Allen alive and educate the laity in the full program of the Church.

items.label: FOR WOMEN
items.title: Women's Missionary Society
items.body: As women called to discipleship — growing in knowledge of God through Jesus Christ, committed to support the mission of the Church, and empowered by the Holy Spirit — we are challenged to help one another engage in ministry and action.

items.label: FOR YOUTH
items.title: Young People's Division
items.body: Under the leadership of the Women's Missionary Society, the YPD provides meaningful youth training and leadership experience in all areas of church life — evangelism, Christian social relations, and education programs.

items.label: FOR MEN
items.title: Sons of Allen
items.body: Our mission is to reach far and wide to bring men to Christ, to organize and train men for the work of the ministry, to teach the history of our church, and to share our faith in the context of our daily lives.

education.lede: Our Department of Christian Education develops comprehensive programs that lead to knowledge of the Holy Scriptures and the Christian Religion — and the implementation of those values in daily life.

classes.title: Youth Church School
classes.body: Classes for every age level, 5 years through 12th grade. Led by dedicated volunteers using reformed curriculum resources. Biblically based activities that help our children and youth creatively wonder about God's story and explore ways to share God's love.

classes.title: Adult Church School
classes.body: A variety of topics and teaching styles designed to meet the needs of adults who continue to study and seek to grow a faith relevant to their daily living. Come study God's Word in community.

classes.title: Morning Worship
classes.body: In-person at 3 Waterworks Road and streamed live on Instagram. A time to gather, to praise, to pray, and to be fed for the week ahead. All are welcome.

connectional.lede: The A.M.E. Church operates in the doctrinal tradition of the Methodist Church with a hierarchical leadership structure. At Bethel, two primary boards work collaboratively under the Pastor's leadership to ensure the ministry integrity of our church.

items.label: SPIRITUAL FORMATION
items.name: Steward Board
items.body: Responsible for the spiritual formation of the body of Christ — ensuring the church is always moving, dwelling, and being on one accord with the principles of Christ. An extension of the Pastor, fostering spiritual maturity, prayer, and empowerment.

items.label: FINANCIAL STEWARDSHIP
items.name: Steward Board (Finance)
items.body: Responsible for the financial health of the physical church — ensuring finances parallel the church's growth, meeting the needs of the Pastor and family, and serving the congregation, building, and community at large.

items.label: PROPERTY & CARE
items.name: Trustee Board
items.body: Nominated by the Pastor and voted annually by the church membership, amenable to the Quarterly Conference. The Board of Trustees is responsible for the maintenance, upkeep, planning, and oversight of all church properties.

items.label: COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIPS
items.name: Beyond the Walls
items.body: We foster partnerships with state and local government and grassroots organizations to ensure the necessary appropriation of services to the poor, the needy, and the disenfranchised — walking worthy of the calling toward kingdom building.

paragraphs: Bethel is part of a global denomination. The African Methodist Episcopal Church is divided into 19 Episcopal Districts — a worldwide church with congregations in South America, Europe, Africa, Canada, Bermuda, the Caribbean Islands, and the United States.

Founded in 1787 by Rev. Richard Allen — the first leader and a former slave — the A.M.E. Church was born out of the fight for dignity and equal worship. We are proud to be part of such a rich history and heritage, and we welcome others to join us in carrying on this tremendous legacy.

giving.verseText: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

ways.title: Give Online
ways.body: Offerings can be made through our Givify account or through our SUBSPLASH giving platform. Secure, simple, and convenient — from anywhere.

ways.title: Give in Person
ways.body: Tithes and offerings are collected within the worship service experience. Special gifts and gifts for Missions are also received during service. Thank you for giving.

ways.title: Mail a Check
ways.body: Place your check in a church envelope, indicate the purpose on the memo (Tithe or Offering), make payable to Bethel AME Church, and mail to: 3 Waterworks Road, Freehold, NJ 07728.

contactForm.title: Get in Touch
contactForm.lede: We'd love to hear from you. Fill out the form below and a member of our team will be in touch.
contactForm.note: We respond to every message. Thank you for reaching out.

prayerForm.title: Prayer Requests
prayerForm.lede: We would be honored to pray for you or someone you know. Our prayer team prays for every request we receive.
prayerForm.note: Your request is held in confidence by our prayer team.

resources.lede: Local organizations we trust to serve the needs of our neighbors in Freehold and Monmouth County.

items.name: Mercy Center
items.body: Serving families in need throughout Monmouth County.

items.name: Open Door Food Pantry
items.body: Providing food and essentials to our community.

items.name: Provisions Promised Pantry
items.body: Meeting the needs of neighbors with dignity.

items.name: SCAN NJ
items.body: Senior Citizens Activities Network — support and community for NJ seniors.

closing.text: So you see, faith by itself isn't enough. Unless it produces good deeds, it is dead and useless.

events.lede: From worship and fellowship to community service and celebration — here's what's happening at Bethel.

featured.title: Annual Holiday Gala
featured.location: Bethel AME Church · 3 Waterworks Road, Freehold, NJ
featured.description: Join us for our annual Holiday Gala — an evening of celebration, fellowship, and community. Details to come as we get closer to the date.

events.pastLede: A look back at recent gatherings, celebrations, and community moments at Bethel.

past.title: 2024 Annual Holiday Gala
past.description: A beautiful evening of fellowship, music, and celebration with the Bethel family and friends from across the community.

past.title: 2023 Annual Holiday Gala
past.description: Our annual gala bringing together the Bethel congregation for an evening of joy, gratitude, and togetherness.
